preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 74%, giving the team a commanding edge in a matchup played on neutral ground. Algeria's modeled win probability stands at just 5%, roughly one-seventh of Argentina's chances.

The W/D/L spread reveals a heavily skewed contest. Argentina's three-to-one advantage over a draw outcome suggests the model sees a competitive gap but does not rule out a stalemate; draws are plausible in roughly one in five scenarios. Algeria's low win probability indicates the model expects asymmetric pressure, though the team retains a non-negligible tail of outcomes.

The model and sportsbooks diverge notably on Algeria's chances. The market consensus puts the underdog at 11–12%, roughly double the model's 5% assessment. Both sources agree closely on Argentina's win probability (74% model, 67% books) and draw likelihood (21%). The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better captures Algeria's true strength; the discrepancy may reflect market overweighting of uncertainty or the model's confidence in the gap.
